title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward leads Red Sox with 12 stolen bases in 12 spring training games, showcasing impressive base-running skills.
Max Ferguson, displaying versatility, leads Red Sox with 7 RBIs and an OPS of .821, suggesting potential for future call-up.
Tayron Guerrero stands out in bullpen with six strikeouts in six innings, aiming for a valuable relief role in 2026.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second in 5-on-5 goals for the Bruins with 13, securing his role on the team.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney opted against trading Arvidsson, valuing his contribution over potential draft picks.
Arvidsson's expiring contract raises questions, but a short-term extension might stabilize the Bruins' forward group.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears, potentially moving Jared Wilson to center from left guard.
Isaac Seumalo, potential top guard free agent, may interest Patriots but brings cost and age considerations.
Elgton Jenkins, Packers' C/G, could be available if released, offering flexibility and experience for Patriots.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, from Stockton, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Make-A-Wish children.
Perry has been involved with Make-A-Wish for 15 years, meeting many inspiring Wish children during this time.
Perry honors both past and present Make-A-Wish children by raising money and awareness through his marathon run.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots predicted to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe for three years, $48 million, $25 million guaranteed.
Boye Mafe recorded 44 pressures in 2025, with a 17.4% pass rush win rate, second only to Milton Williams.
ESPN predicts Patriots will not sign Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce, who may join Chargers with a $80 million deal.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at the House of Blues in Boston, notable for sarcastic political commentary and country music.
Welles responded to recent events with songs like 'Sometimes You Bomb Iran' and covered classic tracks like 'Paranoid.'
The concert featured a mix of original songs and covers, including 'Creep' by Radiohead and 'Take Me Home, Country Roads.'

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida's solo home run contributed to a strong inning, with Shohei Ohtani and Seiya Suzuki also homering for Japan.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in Japan, where his brother Luke Maye plays basketball for the Kobe Storks.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, a Roslindale native, will run her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
She is raising funds for Mass Eye and Ear, credited for diagnosing her two eye conditions.
Aboujaoude-Quirk works as a teacher in Boston Public Schools and aims to support healthcare research.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler, ESPN's longest-tenured NBA analyst since 2000, joins the top NBA broadcast team with Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler, 59, was promoted to ESPN/ABC's premier NBA broadcast spot in August 2025, replacing Doc Rivers and JJ Redick.
Legler expresses excitement about analyzing Celtics' progress, praising Joe Mazzulla's coaching and anticipating Jayson Tatum's return from injury.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics 298 days after tearing his Achilles, scoring 15 points in a win over the Mavericks.
Cooper Flagg made his NBA debut at TD Garden, scoring 16 points for the Mavericks in a 120-100 loss to the Celtics.
A large contingent from Maine attended to support Flagg, a former high school star in Newport now with Dallas.
